hero.name: Erwin
hero.tagline: Building AI products that make job-seeking smarter and faster

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: AI product & full-stack experience, data-driven mindset
about.description: I'm Wang Zihan (Erwin), holding an M.S. in Corporate Strategy & Management from EDHEC Business School (Financial Times European Top 10) and a B.S. in Marketing from Jiangxi University of Finance & Economics (top 5%). Currently working as an AI Product Manager, focused on designing and shipping AI Agent products. I excel at translating complex business problems into executable AI solutions, with strengths in SQL analytics, A/B experimentation, and full-stack development. Certified in Lean Agile (Scrum, SAFe 6.0), ACCA, and CFA Level I.

iterationSummary.title: Iteration Strategy Summary
iterationSummary.stage1_summary: Transformed open-ended assignment requirements into clear product goals, target audience, and minimum viable solution.
iterationSummary.stage1_bullet1: Designed browsing paths for recruiters and interviewers
iterationSummary.stage1_bullet2: Defined website goal as "quickly building awareness and trust"
iterationSummary.stage1_bullet3: Determined tech stack, section structure, and UX flow through multiple AI conversations
iterationSummary.stage2_summary: Replaced generic template content with structured personal data, making the website go from "viewable" to "credible".
iterationSummary.stage2_bullet1: Added realProfile.json as content data source
iterationSummary.stage2_bullet2: Mapped education, experience, projects, and skills to real sections
iterationSummary.stage2_bullet3: Completed bilingual, dark mode, and first runnable site
iterationSummary.stage3_summary: Added an AI assistant constrained by personal data, allowing visitors to learn about me through conversation, not just browsing.
iterationSummary.stage3_bullet2: Built knowledge base, system prompt, and API route
iterationSummary.stage3_bullet3: Constrained responses strictly around personal data and website context
iterationSummary.stage4_summary: After the first runnable version, continued optimizing information architecture, navigation, and interaction priorities to make it feel like a complete product, not just a demo.
iterationSummary.stage4_bullet2: Split long content into separate pages
iterationSummary.stage4_bullet3: Adjusted AI assistant position, navigation structure, and reading flow
iterationSummary.takeaway: This isn't a one-time generated page, but a product that gradually converged through multiple rounds of "define-generate-evaluate-iterate".

aiMethod.title: Website Iteration Process
aiMethod.subtitle: From MVP to polished product
aiMethod.intro: This isn't just a resume website, but a productized personal brand portal built through AI iteration.
aiMethod.metrics3: AI-assisted process from planning to deployment
aiMethod.section1_intro: In this project, AI wasn't just a "code generator" but a collaborative tool that participated in problem definition, structure planning, page implementation, feature expansion, and continuous optimization.
aiMethod.logic1_title: Define audience first, then define pages
aiMethod.logic1_body: I first clarified that the website serves recruiters and interviewers, so the focus isn't "showing more" but "letting them quickly understand who I am, what I've done, and how I think".
aiMethod.logic2_body: Before code generation, I used AI to output site structure, section hierarchy, and UX flow, ensuring the website has clear information architecture rather than just stacking pages.
aiMethod.logic3_title: Constrain content with real data first
aiMethod.logic3_body: After the first version, I added structured personal data, replacing generic copy with realProfile.json to make page content more aligned with my actual background.
aiMethod.logic4_title: Launch MVP first, then continue iterating
aiMethod.logic4_body: I didn't aim for perfection in one shot. Instead, I completed a runnable version first, then continued optimizing narrative, interaction, navigation, and long-form content structure.
aiMethod.phase1_paragraph: Initially, I broke down the assignment requirements and transformed them into a clearer product question: who is this website for, what do they need to see most in the shortest time, and what experience best establishes professionalism and trust.
aiMethod.phase1_what_changed:
Defined target users as recruiters and interviewers
Set overall style as minimal, professional, trustworthy
Used Gemini, Trae, and other tools to help produce prompts, tech stack, and site plan
aiMethod.phase1_why_mattered: Without defining audience and success criteria upfront, the generated website would easily be just a template page, not a product with clear expression goals.
aiMethod.phase1_outputs:
Product goal definition
Tech stack direction
Section structure and UX flow
MVP plan
aiMethod.phase2_paragraph: After the first generated version, I found generic content weakened credibility, so I added structured personal data and reorganized pages around real content.
aiMethod.phase2_what_changed:
Added realProfile.json
Replaced template content with education, experience, projects, and skills
Completed first bilingual, dark mode, and core page structure
aiMethod.phase2_why_mattered: Whether the website "feels like me" largely depends on whether content is real, specific, and mappable, not whether pages are flashy.
aiMethod.phase2_outputs:
Data-driven content source
First complete site
Bilingual and theme capabilities
Basic section system
aiMethod.phase3_paragraph: To make this website closer to an "AI product manager" positioning, I added an AI assistant constrained by personal data, allowing visitors to quickly understand my background and projects through conversation.
aiMethod.phase3_what_changed:
Integrated Qwen-Plus API
Built portfolio knowledge, system prompt, API route, and chat UI
Constrained model to only answer based on data and website content
aiMethod.phase3_why_mattered: This step upgraded the website from "static display" to "interactive experience", more directly demonstrating how I incorporate AI as part of product capability.
aiMethod.phase3_outputs:
AI conversation module
Knowledge base and prompt structure
Backend interface
Recruiting-scenario oriented suggested questions
aiMethod.phase4_paragraph: After features were working, I continued treating it as a product to optimize, including content layering, navigation organization, reading paths, interaction priorities, and overall completeness.
aiMethod.phase4_what_changed:
Added Gallery and Timeline
Split AI methodology, resume, and contact into separate pages
Adjusted homepage structure and navigation logic
Optimized AI assistant style, language switching, and project display organization
aiMethod.phase4_why_mattered: A working demo doesn't equal a highly complete product. The focus of subsequent polish was making information access smoother, reading cost lower, and overall impression more stable.
aiMethod.phase4_outputs:
Hybrid page architecture
Clearer navigation hierarchy
Better interaction priorities
More complete product expression
aiMethod.section3_intro: Different tools played different roles in this project: some helped me break down problems, some helped organize solutions, and some directly advanced code generation and feature implementation.
aiMethod.workflow1: Gemini: Used for early goal clarification, prompt drafting, and site concept exploration
aiMethod.workflow2: Trae IDE + GLM 5 / Qwen: Used for generating implementation plans, organizing tech solutions, and site structure
aiMethod.workflow3: Cursor + Claude: Used for code generation, component iteration, page refactoring, and feature expansion
aiMethod.workflow4: Next.js + Tailwind CSS: As the final product implementation framework
aiMethod.workflow5: Qwen-Plus API: As the model capability layer for homepage AI assistant
aiMethod.section4_intro: The comparison area below shows the evolution from early versions to current version. Currently placeholders are retained, to be replaced with real screenshots later.
aiMethod.compare1_title: From Generic Structure to Recruiter-Oriented Personal Brand Portal
aiMethod.compare1_caption: Key comparison: From "standard personal homepage" to productized structure emphasizing target audience, expression efficiency, and trust.
aiMethod.compare2_title: From Static Display to AI Interactive Experience
aiMethod.compare2_caption: Key comparison: Added conversation entry, allowing visitors to quickly understand personal background and project highlights through questions.
aiMethod.compare3_title: From Long Single-Page to Hybrid Information Architecture
aiMethod.compare3_caption: Key comparison: Split long content out, reducing homepage burden, improving reading flow and navigation clarity.
aiMethod.placeholder_label: Placeholder, to be replaced with real screenshots
aiMethod.section5_body: This website wasn't generated in one shot, but gradually converged through multiple rounds of "define-generate-evaluate-optimize" under real goal constraints. For me, AI's most valuable aspect isn't replacing judgment, but amplifying judgment and iteration efficiency.

aiChat.title: Ask My AI
aiChat.subtitle: An intelligent assistant trained on my real background — ask anything
aiChat.assistant_desc: Answers based on portfolio data & project experience
aiChat.welcome: Hi! I'm Erwin's AI assistant. Feel free to ask me about his experience and projects.
aiChat.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
aiChat.max_turns_reached: You've reached the maximum conversation limit. Please tip Erwin 1 yuan 0v0
aiChat.q2: What AI projects has he worked on?
aiChat.q3: What did he do at ByteDance?
aiChat.q5: What were his results at the job AI startup?

timeline.title: Experience Timeline
timeline.subtitle: From marketing to AI product manager — the journey so far
timeline.t1_title: Jiangxi University of Finance & Economics · Marketing
timeline.t1_desc: Studied marketing, project investment, and brand strategy. Served as class secretary, led multiple student activities. Graduated as Outstanding Graduate (top 5%), earning First-Class Academic Scholarship and National-level entrepreneurship project lead.
timeline.t2_subtitle: Student Club · Coding competitions & tech talks
timeline.t2_desc: Served as director of the campus tech club, organizing coding competitions and tech sharing sessions. Built team management and developer community skills that laid the groundwork for future AI and product work.
timeline.t3_desc: Led a team to develop brand strategy and marketing plans for partner liquor companies, conducted field research visits, pitched in Kunming, Yunnan, and participated in city exhibitions. Project received national-level approval.
timeline.t4_subtitle: Internet+ Beijing Silver · Global Brand Strategy 1st Prize · E-Commerce Provincial 2nd
timeline.t4_desc: Based on the national entrepreneurship project, won the Internet+ Innovation Competition Beijing Silver Award, the Global Brand Strategy Competition National 1st Prize, and the National College E-Commerce Competition Provincial 2nd Prize.
timeline.t5_title: EDHEC Business School (France) · M.S. Corporate Strategy
timeline.t5_subtitle: Financial Times European Top 10 · Management Top 20%
timeline.t5_desc: Pursued a master's in Corporate Strategy & Management in France, studying strategic management, corporate finance, and international business. Earned Louis Vuitton INSIDE and Bocconi VC&PE certifications while actively developing AI and data analysis skills.
timeline.t6_desc: Maintained Airtable employee database with 98%+ accuracy, assisted social media promotion, and built 3 core business automation workflows using Apollo, improving daily efficiency by ~30%.
timeline.t7_desc: Used Mixpanel funnel analysis to identify NFT payment drop-off points, ran A/B tests to optimize payment UX, simplified fiat payment flow and added crypto wallet onboarding steps. Conversion rate improved 4%, payment success rate up 7%.
timeline.t8_desc: Drove TikTok North America search optimization through case evaluation, data analysis, and A/B experiments (opms +0.5%). Independently designed product card trust labels (pv ctr A/B +1%) and led recommendation strategy optimization for full rollout.
timeline.t9_title: Job Application AI · AI Product Manager
timeline.t9_desc: Led AI auto-apply Agent design and iteration, upgrading architecture from static DOM parsing to Vision-agent loop multimodal decision-making. Dynamically adapted to 200+ company application pages with 92%+ fill accuracy and 30x user efficiency improvement.
timeline.t10_desc: Built a context-aware conversational AI Agent using RAG, with sentence-transformers + LanceDB for a local dialogue knowledge base and Qwen3 for two-stage reasoning, exploring AI-powered social connection.
timeline.t11_desc: Enrolled in a Computer Science & AI bachelor's degree online, systematically studying SQL, Python, software engineering, machine learning, intelligent agents, RAG, and fine-tuning to continuously strengthen AI engineering capabilities.
timeline.t12_title: Google Developer Hackathon · AI Business Card Generator
timeline.t12_desc: Built an end-to-end personal information structuring engine supporting any resume format → structured JSON → normalized PDF → Agent conversation. Led multimodal parsing design using text extraction + OCR + lightweight 7B model layered architecture.
timeline.t13_title: Shenzhen Hackathon (Douyin) · EV AI Sales Assistant
timeline.t13_desc: Designed an AI consulting platform for EV purchasing decisions built on Dify, integrating an AI chatbot for smart Q&A and personalized recommendations based on budget, range, and charging conditions.
